title: helianthemum dubium
intro: Helianthemum dubium, also known as Rockrose, is a perennial shrub native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its fragrant, pink or white flowers and dense, woody stems. Traditionally, Helianthemum dubium has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are derived from its flavonoids and terpenoids. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving immune function, and potentially acting as an antimicrobial agent. Helianthemum dubium can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its effectiveness and safety as a medicinal agent have not been extensively scientifically studied.
